title: Disambiguate memory into HBM / DRAM / other memory classes
summary: A generic memory fact edge should be expanded only after filings or supplier disclosures explicitly mention HBM, DRAM, GDDR, NAND, or another memory subtype.

title: Check whether disclosed memory supply includes conventional DRAM
summary: The generic memory edge can cover several memory classes; DRAM should remain a research lead until exact product wording appears in a source.

title: Trace HBM into advanced packaging capacity
summary: HBM exposure normally requires packaging capacity research, but the buyer-specific chain needs official packaging, OSAT, or foundry evidence.

title: Trace foundry exposure into silicon wafer supply
summary: A foundry/wafer fact edge implies a need to research silicon wafer suppliers, but not a company-specific wafer supplier relation without direct evidence.

title: Trace advanced wafer production into lithography equipment constraints
summary: Advanced-node wafer supply depends on lithography capacity, but equipment exposure is a capacity/risk context until official disclosures link a company or facility.

title: Track photoresist and specialty chemistry exposure
summary: Wafer fabrication creates upstream chemical exposure; this is a material-chain lead until supported by supplier, facility, or import evidence.

title: Track specialty gas constraints for wafer fabrication
summary: Specialty gases are a wafer fabrication dependency; public sources usually expose this as facility or regional risk rather than direct buyer-supplier fact.

title: Trace advanced packaging into substrate capacity
summary: Advanced packaging research should check ABF substrate and interposer constraints before asserting any company-specific bottleneck.

title: Trace advanced packaging into interposer supply
summary: Interposers can be a bottleneck in advanced packages, but they must remain a research lead without direct packaging evidence.

title: Trace contract manufacturing into PCB and board-level suppliers
summary: EMS relationships create board-level supply exposure; official BOM or supplier-list evidence is needed before writing company-specific PCB edges.

title: Trace AI server manufacturing into power supply exposure
summary: AI server assembly has power supply dependencies; keep this as a lead until supplier lists, official BOMs, or procurement evidence confirm a relation.

title: Trace AI server manufacturing into cooling exposure
summary: Thermal components can be material for accelerated systems, but buyer-specific links need official supplier or facility evidence.

title: Trace manufacturing services into export routes and ports
summary: Finished goods logistics should be modeled as route observations or leads; AIS and trade data do not prove cargo ownership by themselves.
